'''Joe Brown''' (born 14 January 1999) is an English [[rugby league]] footballer who plays as a {{rlp|wg}} or {{rlp|fb}} for the [[Sheffield Eagles]] in the [[RFL Championship|Championship]].<ref name="Match Report">{{cite web|url=https://www.bradfordbulls.co.uk/squads/player/185/joe-brown|title=Player Profile|publisher=Bradford Bulls|date=21 February 2021|access-date=21 February 2021}}</ref>

==Background==
Brown was born in [[Leeds]], [[West Yorkshire]], England. He was brought up in the youth system at the Bulls, making him a product of the [[Bradford Bulls Academy]], until he signed for [[Wigan Warriors|Wigan]]'s academy in 2017.<ref name="Brown Signs for Wigan">{{cite web|url=https://www.wigantoday.net/sport/rugby-league/browns-been-wigans-radar-while-745919|title=Brown's been on Wigan's radar for a while|publisher=Wigan Today|date=25 January 2017|access-date=21 February 2021}}</ref>

However he re-signed with his former club Bradford in May 2019 for the first team on an 18-month contract.<ref name="Brown Signs for Bradford">{{cite web|url=https://www.rugby-league.com/article/54986/bradford-sign-former-england-academy-star|title=BRADFORD SIGN FORMER ENGLAND ACADEMY STAR|publisher=RFL|date=25 May 2019|access-date=21 February 2021}}</ref>

==Playing career==
===Bradford Bulls===
'''2019''' - [[2019 Bradford Bulls season|2019 Season]]

Brown made his debut in the [[2019 RFL 1895 Cup]] Round 2 against [[Barrow Raiders|Barrow]]. He then featured in Round 22 against [[York City Knights|York City]] before picking up an injury.

'''2020''' - [[2020 Bradford Bulls season|2020 Season]]

Brown featured in the pre-season friendlies against the [[Castleford Tigers]], [[Leeds Rhinos|Leeds]], [[Dewsbury Rams|Dewsbury]] and York City. He scored two tries against Castleford.

Brown featured in Round 1 against the [[London Broncos]] to Round 5 [[Sheffield Eagles|Sheffield]]. He also featured in the [[2020 Challenge Cup]] from Round 4 against the [[Underbank Rangers]] to Round 5 [[Wakefield Trinity]].

At the end of the season, Brown signed a one-year extension to stay at Bradford.<ref name="Brown Signs Extension">{{cite web|url=https://www.thetelegraphandargus.co.uk/sport/18619346.versatile-brown-signs-new-one-year-deal-bradford-bulls/|title=Versatile Brown signs new one-year deal with Bradford Bulls|publisher=Telegraph & Argus|date=31 July 2020|access-date=21 February 2021}}</ref>

'''2021''' - [[2021 Bradford Bulls season|2021 Season]]

Brown featured in the pre-season friendly against [[Swinton Lions|Swinton]]. Brown featured in Round 1 against Sheffield to Round 9 against [[Featherstone Rovers]] then in Round 11 [[Widnes Vikings|Widnes]] to Round 18 [[Swinton Lions|Swinton]]. He also featured in Round 20 against [[Toulouse Olympique]] to Elimination Playoff [[Batley Bulldogs|Batley]]. Brown also played in the [[2021 Challenge Cup]] in Round 1 against Featherstone.

===York Knights RLFC===
On 7 June 2025, Brown played in York's 1895 Cup final victory over [[Featherstone Rovers|Featherstone]].<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/articles/cm26dp2l3vvo |title=York pip Featherstone in extra time to win 1895 Cup |work=BBC Sport |date=7 June 2025 |access-date=8 June 2025}}</ref>

===Sheffield Eagles===
On 11 October 2025 it was reported that he had signed for [[Sheffield Eagles]] on a 2-year deal<ref>{{cite web|website=Total RL|author=Doug Thomson|date=11 Oct 2025|title=Sheffield Eagles sign prolific winger from York Knights|url=https://www.totalrl.com/sheffield-eagles-sign-prolific-winger-from-york-knights/}}</ref>
